articles.title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
articles.summary: Elgato's Stream Deck 7.4 update now supports Model Context Protocol, enabling AI assistants like Claude and ChatGPT to control Stream Deck buttons automatically through voice or text commands.

articles.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
articles.summary: Baidu's robotaxis malfunctioned in Wuhan, freezing on highways and trapping passengers, causing traffic chaos and at least one accident.

articles.title: Claude Code leak exposes a Tamagotchi-style ‘pet’ and an always-on agent
articles.summary: Claude Code's 2.1.88 update accidentally leaked over 512,000 lines of TypeScript source code through an exposed source map file, revealing internal details about the AI coding tool's functionality.

articles.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
articles.summary: Anthropic accidentally issued thousands of GitHub takedown notices for leaked source code. The company retracted most notices, calling the mass removal an unintended mistake.

articles.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
articles.summary: Meta's Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants, significantly increasing energy consumption in South Dakota.

articles.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
articles.summary: Cognichip raised $60M to use AI for designing AI chips, claiming it can reduce development costs by 75% and cut timelines in half.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid growing demand for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring stunt featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment strategy helped the startup attract engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent whitepapers show quantum computers require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption, with neutral atom approaches achieving 100x overhead reduction and Google demonstrating blockchain encryption breaking in under 9 minutes.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google accelerated its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current encryption. The company urges global adoption of post-quantum cryptography to protect sensitive data.

articles.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
articles.summary: TeamPCP, a hacking group, deploys self-propagating malware targeting unsecured cloud platforms for data theft, ransomware, and cryptocurrency mining. The group also uses a data wiper specifically targeting Iranian machines.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers worldwide are training humanoid robots by recording themselves performing daily tasks with smartphones. Companies like Micro1 collect this video data to train AI-powered robots, employing thousands across 50+ countries.

articles.title: The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home
articles.summary: Gig workers like Zeus in Nigeria record themselves performing tasks at home to provide training data for humanoid robots. Companies like Tesla and Figure AI use this crowdsourced video data to train robots designed for factories and homes.

articles.title: Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
articles.summary: LLM capability gains have plateaued, but domain-specialized and customized models show significant improvements. Organizations gain competitive advantage by customizing AI models with proprietary data and internal logic.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use technology, advancing how AI systems interact with digital interfaces and expanding capabilities in automation and human-computer interaction.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception develops advanced computer vision technology for autonomous systems. The platform enables real-time object detection and scene understanding across various applications.

articles.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents
articles.summary: IBM releases Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a compact multimodal AI model designed for enterprise document processing. The lightweight model combines vision and language capabilities for efficient document analysis.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impact. The conversation explores upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available. The release aims to advance sustainable farming practices through accessible artificial intelligence tools.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google and Brazil's government created a satellite imagery map to monitor and protect Brazilian forests from deforestation and illegal activities.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in March 2026, showcasing new developments and features across their AI product suite and research initiatives.

articles.title: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model
articles.summary: Google launches Veo 3.1 Lite, a cost-effective video generation model available through Gemini API and Google AI Studio for paid preview and testing.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a new voice model with improved precision and lower latency for more natural, fluid, and precise voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect users from harmful AI behaviors.

articles.title: Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more
articles.summary: Google introduces Lyria 3 Pro, enabling creation of longer music tracks with improved structural awareness. The technology is expanding to more Google products and platforms.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The podcast discusses these releases alongside Mamba 3 and attention residuals developments in AI.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions to generate detailed visuals.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KPMG: Inside the AI agent playbook driving enterprise margin gains
articles.summary: Despite $186M average AI spending, only 11% of enterprises deploy AI agents at scale. While 64% report meaningful outcomes, most see incremental gains rather than margin-moving operational efficiency.

articles.title: DeepL’s Borderless Business report reveals 83% of enterprises are still behind on language AI
articles.summary: DeepL's 2026 report reveals 83% of enterprises lag in language AI adoption despite widespread AI investment. Translation workflows remain significantly underautomated across sales, legal, and customer support operations.

articles.title: Hershey applies AI across its supply chain operations
articles.summary: Hershey Company is implementing AI across its supply chain, from ingredient sourcing to plant automation and product fulfillment, aiming to create a faster and more resilient operation.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to harvest credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 model advances video self-supervised learning by successfully balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a major challenge in AI development.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering combined with parabolic extrapolation transformed an LLM into a theoretical collaborator, producing a testable multiverse model for scientific discovery.
